title: China conducts combat readiness patrols in Scarborough Shoal
summary: China's military said its naval and air forces conducted combat readiness patrols in the Scarborough Shoal and surrounding disputed areas.
contents:
# China conducts combat readiness patrols in Scarborough Shoal

## Overview
China's military said its naval and air forces conducted combat readiness patrols in the Scarborough Shoal and surrounding disputed areas. China claimed it expelled Philippine aircraft and vessels near the disputed atolls as part of these operations.

## Reporting Window
- Reported timeframe: 2026-04-30
- Location: Scarborough Shoal, South China Sea

## Key Details
- China's naval and air forces conducted the patrols on April 30, 2026
- The patrols got described by China's Southern Theater Command as "combat readiness" operations
- China characterized the patrols as a "countermeasure to cope with all sorts of rights-violation and provocative acts"
- The operations occurred amid joint Philippine-US exercises in the region
- China stated the patrols got "meant to resolutely safeguard China's territorial sovereignty and uphold peace and stability in the South China Sea"
- The Philippine embassy in Beijing did not immediately respond to requests for comment

## Source Context
The @CoastGuardPH account on X (Twitter) covers Philippine Coast Guard's subject area as documented in this repository's source manifest. The development above appears in news reporting that aligns with the topical scope this account monitors. Verification stems from the cited publication, not from a direct tweet capture (Twitter API access remained blocked during this collection cycle).
